speakers.name: P. Ramakrishnan
speakers.role: Head, Investor Relations, Larsen & Toubro Limited
key_topics: economic conditions in India and Middle East
direct_answers: So Mohit, I think while I was concluding my presentation, I gave an update on the Revenue guidance itself. Q4 has always been the most busiest quarter for the Projects & Manufacturing business portfolio. So we continue to retain our guidance of 15% for the full year, and we are reasonably confident that Q4, the way we have planned, the execution momentum will be at a fast forward pace, both for the Infrastructure -- for all the segments in the Projects & Manufacturing space. That is baked in.
dodged_or_vague: Can you repeat that question, Aditya, please?
speakers.summary: P. Ramakrishnan opened the earnings call with a confident overview of L&T's Q3 FY '26 performance, highlighting strong order inflows and revenue growth. He emphasized the resilience of the Indian economy and the strategic importance of the Middle East market. Ramakrishnan provided detailed financial metrics, including a significant improvement in the Net Working Capital to Revenue ratio. He maintained a confident tone throughout, particularly when discussing the company's ability to meet its full-year revenue growth guidance. While he directly answered questions about revenue guidance, he was less specific when asked about certain order details.

speakers.name: Subramanian Sarma
speakers.role: Deputy Managing Director and President, Larsen & Toubro Limited
direct_answers: No, it means that we have been selected for the whole program, right? But then, timing-wise, the customer has to decide when he wants to call up which project. So, we'll have to wait. But I think when they call up, then we'll have a secured position. But until he calls out, as a prudent policy, we are not counting it.
dodged_or_vague: I mean it's very difficult to put a number because it depends on what happens in the Middle East in relation to what happens in the domestic. I mean I think the good news is that I think we are growing well, and we'll continue to grow. I think we are very confident about it.
speakers.summary: Subramanian Sarma expressed optimism about L&T's prospects in the Middle East, highlighting a strong pipeline of opportunities in countries like Saudi Arabia, Qatar, and the UAE. He noted the positive developments in the thermal power plant sector and anticipated significant growth in this area over the next two years. Sarma was confident about L&T's strategic positioning and its ability to secure future projects, although he was cautious about counting unconfirmed orders. While he provided direct answers regarding the company's selection for major programs, he was less specific when discussing potential growth figures.

speakers.name: Moderator
speakers.summary: The moderator facilitated the Q3 FY '26 earnings conference call for Larsen & Toubro, ensuring that the call proceeded smoothly and that participants were aware of the listen-only mode and the process for asking questions. The moderator maintained a neutral tone throughout, focusing on logistical aspects of the call. They concluded the session by thanking participants and signaling the end of the conference.
